legendsBattlegroundsBeginnerGuide.title: Legends Battlegrounds Beginner Guide
legendsBattlegroundsBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Build reliable movement, defense and combo habits before chasing long damage strings. This first-session route uses Martial Legend as the simplest starting fighter.
legendsBattlegroundsBeginnerGuide.intro: Legends Battlegrounds is a fast Roblox arena fighter built around light attacks, dashes, blocking, ragdoll escapes and awakening moves. Start by learning when to stop attacking: blocked pressure and a wasted evasive are two of the fastest ways to lose a fight.

steps.title: Choose a First Fighter
points: Select Martial Legend for a direct, balanced kit with a counter, a guard-breaking palm strike and clear combo extenders.
Move to Jun after you can consistently dash around blocks and track an opponent's evasive.
Use Shinji once you are comfortable with rushdown timing, because most of his base moves can be blocked.

steps.title: Drill the Core Controls
points: Memorize the official controls card so dash, block, evasive and awakening are always one key away.
Perform ten dashes, ten blocks and five ragdoll cancels until they feel automatic.
Keep one finger near Q and F so you can react to pressure the moment it starts.

steps.title: Practice Movement Before Attacking
points: Use side dashes to change angle instead of repeatedly dashing straight into an opponent's block.
Save the forward dash for closing distance after a knockback or catching a missed move.
Circle at medium range and watch whether the opponent spends Q first.

steps.title: Build One Defensive Rule
points: Hold F against predictable M1 pressure, then release block before repositioning.
Use Q while ragdolled only when the opponent still has moves ready to extend the combo.
After spending evasive, play farther away until it returns instead of immediately re-engaging.

steps.title: Learn Two Universal Starters
points: Uppercut starter: 3 M1 into Uppercut into a character move keeps the target close for launch-catch options.
Downslam starter: 3 M1 into Downslam into a ground-hitting move tests whether the opponent will spend evasive.
Run each starter three times without mashing the next move early.

steps.title: Run a First Martial Legend Route
points: Sequence: Forward Dash, 3 M1, Uppercut, Panther Combo, Dragon Rush, Forward Dash, 3 M1, Uppercut, Tiger Palm.
Pause briefly after launchers so Dragon Rush and the next forward dash meet the opponent as they fall.
If the opponent evades after Panther Combo or an uppercut, stop the route, block and punish the next approach.

steps.title: Activate Awakening With a Plan
points: Press G only when the awakening bar is ready.
Create space with a knockback before activating so the opponent cannot immediately pressure you.
Use awakening to secure a fight, not as a panic button after every cooldown has been spent.

steps.title: Avoid Common Beginner Mistakes
points: Forward-dashing into block: side dash, wait for a swing, then start M1s.
Spending evasive on the first knockdown: check whether the opponent can still extend before pressing Q.
Using every move at once: keep one move ready to punish a missed dash or failed combo.
Repeating the same opener: alternate block pressure, side-dash M1 and a delayed counter bait.

quickTips: Stop attacking into a held block and look for a side dash instead.
Track the opponent's evasive before committing a long string.
Keep one cooldown ready to punish a missed move.
Practice one route until it is reliable before learning the next.

legendsBattlegroundsTierList.title: Legends Battlegrounds Tier List
legendsBattlegroundsTierList.subtitle: The standard roster centers on Martial Legend, Jun and Shinji. The rankings below separate ease of use from maximum PvP potential.
legendsBattlegroundsTierList.intro: Martial Legend is the cleanest character for learning neutral play, Jun has the highest technical combo ceiling, and Shinji specializes in mobility, multi-target pressure and Flashstep Fury resets. Matchup knowledge matters more than raw tier placement once both players can track evasive and cooldowns.

entries.summary: The kit is straightforward: Dragon Rush chases, Tiger Palm bypasses guard and hits ragdolled targets, Panther Combo launches, and Triple Kick Counter punishes predictable attacks.
Glimpse of Power bypasses guard, Fang Dance closes distance and Virgule Cascade has multiple routes, but Jun loses value when spacing and variant timing are not controlled.
Body Flicker and Flashstep Fury create strong chase pressure, but most base moves are blockable and Body Flicker can send the target in an awkward direction.
Jun can convert one clean opening into layered pressure through Fang Dance, Wolf Fang Flash movement tech and the Glimpse of Power into Virgule Cascade variant.
Triple Kick Counter can interrupt committed offense, while Tiger Palm and strong wall routes keep the character dependable against passive and aggressive opponents.
Nunchuck Flurry or Body Flicker can refresh Flashstep Fury through the reset mechanic, creating repeated pressure, but disciplined blocking limits several openers.

legendsBattlegroundsBestCombos.title: Legends Battlegrounds Best Combos
legendsBattlegroundsBestCombos.subtitle: Use the shorter routes in live matches first, then add movement tech and reset mechanics. Every long string becomes safer after the opponent has already spent evasive.
legendsBattlegroundsBestCombos.intro: M1 means a light attack. Uppercuts keep targets airborne, downslams test ragdoll escape timing, and side dashes help realign after launchers. Do not force the next move when the target uses Q while ragdolled; reset to block and punish instead.
legendsBattlegroundsBestCombos.notation: M1 = light attack. Stop the route and block if the target uses evasive.

combos.name: Basic Launch Route
combos.timing: Let the target begin falling after Panther Combo before Dragon Rush connects, then forward dash just before the Dragon Rush knockback ends.
combos.useCase: Open-space 1v1 damage with all four base moves available.

combos.name: Counter Conversion
combos.timing: Side dash immediately after the counter's final kick, then aim Dragon Rush at the target's landing path.
combos.useCase: Punishing predictable Fang Dance, M1 pressure or an aggressive dash.

combos.name: Wall Carry Route
combos.timing: Start near a wall or tree and dash as Panther Combo finishes so the target reaches the wall-combo trigger.
combos.useCase: Short arenas and fights near solid map geometry.

combos.name: Fang Dance Confirm
combos.timing: Use the side dash to line up the forward dash after Fang Dance sends the opponent away.
combos.useCase: A stable route that teaches Jun's chase spacing.

combos.name: Wolf Fang Miss Tech
combos.timing: Angle slightly sideways during the flip so Wolf Fang Flash passes beside the opponent, then side dash back into M1 range.
combos.useCase: Keeping pressure without taking Wolf Fang Flash's normal knockback.

combos.name: Glimpse Cascade Route
combos.timing: Trigger Virgule Cascade during Glimpse of Power's second section to activate the combined kick variant.
combos.useCase: Maximum conversion from a clean opening with key cooldowns ready.

combos.name: Nunchuck Knockdown Route
combos.timing: Use Nunchuck Flurry while the target is down, then aim Lightning Flash as the second downslam lands.
combos.useCase: A simple route ending with Shinji's guard-bypassing base move.

combos.name: Body Flicker Follow-Up
combos.timing: Body Flicker can catch the brief floating state after Nunchuck Flurry, but its random roll direction means you must turn quickly.
combos.useCase: Practicing camera correction and Shinji's two-M1 limit after Body Flicker.

combos.name: Flashstep Reset Route
combos.timing: Using Nunchuck Flurry while Flashstep Fury is unavailable refreshes it through Lightning Rage, allowing the second cast.
combos.useCase: Extended pressure that uses Shinji's signature cooldown-reset mechanic.

legendsBattlegroundsMartialLegend.title: Legends Battlegrounds Martial Legend Guide
legendsBattlegroundsMartialLegend.subtitle: Martial Legend combines simple launchers, a reliable guard break and a punish counter. The character is strongest when you save one move for the opponent's evasive or missed approach.
legendsBattlegroundsMartialLegend.intro: Use Dragon Rush and Panther Combo to carry opponents, Tiger Palm to punish blocking or grounded targets, and Triple Kick Counter against committed attacks. Near walls, Panther Combo can feed directly into Martial Legend's wall sequence for efficient damage.

moves.name: M1 Chain
properties: Final low kick knocks the target away
moves.bestUse: Stop at three M1s before an uppercut or downslam when starting a combo.

moves.name: Dragon Rush
moves.bestUse: Catch the opponent as they fall from Panther Combo, then dash into the landing.

moves.name: Tiger Palm
moves.bestUse: End a combo, punish passive blocking or side dash behind the reduced knockback.

moves.name: Panther Combo
properties: Ends with a launching backflip kick
moves.bestUse: Confirm after an uppercut, then delay Dragon Rush until the target begins to fall.

moves.name: Triple Kick Counter
properties: Long recovery if nothing triggers it
moves.bestUse: Read Fang Dance, an obvious forward dash or repeated M1 pressure.

moves.name: Draconic Instinct
properties: Activates after a successful Triple Kick Counter
moves.bestUse: Use the speed increase to side dash into Dragon Rush or force a fast M1 re-entry.

moves.name: Wall Combo
properties: Knee, body kick, punches and tornado-kick sequence
Needs the opponent carried into solid geometry
moves.bestUse: Near a wall, connect Panther Combo and dash toward the target as the launcher ends.

moves.name: Dragon's Fury
moves.bestUse: Activate after a knockback so the opponent cannot freely pressure the startup.

moves.name: Dragon Blitz
moves.bestUse: Punish a missed move or catch an opponent trying to create distance.

moves.name: Dropping Fang
moves.bestUse: Catch grounded targets or punish opponents grouped near the landing point.

moves.name: Skyfall Bomb
moves.bestUse: Use after a confirmed opening rather than into block or a side dash.

moves.name: Draconic Honor
properties: Carries the target across the ground
moves.bestUse: Aim down a clear lane after the opponent has committed their dash.

moves.name: Against Jun
properties: Hold Triple Kick Counter for Fang Dance
Save evasive for Glimpse of Power into Virgule Cascade
Close with Dragon Rush after a missed Wolf Fang Flash or Fang Dance
moves.bestUse: Force Jun to spend a movement tool first, then take the cleaner close-range exchange.

moves.name: Against Shinji
properties: Block Nunchuck Flurry, Body Flicker and Flashstep Fury used as raw openers
Respect Lightning Flash because it bypasses block
Punish Body Flicker's recovery or Flashstep Fury's startup
moves.bestUse: Stay patient behind block, then convert Shinji's failed rush into Panther Combo pressure.

legendsBattlegroundsJunGuide.title: Legends Battlegrounds Jun Guide
legendsBattlegroundsJunGuide.subtitle: Learn Jun's complete base kit, move variants, wall routes and No More Games awakening. Use confirmed combos to preserve cooldowns and avoid punishable openings.
legendsBattlegroundsJunGuide.intro: Jun is a technical close-range fighter built around confirmed rushes, special move variants and high-damage awakening sequences. His strongest routes come from landing an M1 knockdown or wall combo before committing Fang Dance, Virgule Cascade or Piercing Tempest.

moves.name: Glimpse of Power
moves.description: Jun drives forward with a body strike, rushes through the target and finishes with a kick. The opening hit can catch multiple nearby opponents.
moves.bestUse: Confirm it after an M1 string or downslam instead of throwing it from maximum range.

moves.name: Wolf Fang Flash
moves.guard: Flip blockable, charged strike bypasses block
moves.description: Jun opens with a flipping kick and then launches a fast charged punch with an area hitbox.
moves.bestUse: Save it for chasing an evasive, extending a knockdown or returning to melee range.

moves.name: Fang Dance
moves.guard: Rushing entry can break through guard
moves.description: Jun rushes into a long martial-arts sequence that carries the opponent before launching them away.
moves.bestUse: Use it after an M1 downslam, wall stun or another confirmed hit; a missed attempt leaves Jun open.

moves.name: Virgule Cascade
moves.description: Jun starts with dagger strikes, teleports around the target and ends with a downward area attack. The opening knife hit must connect to trigger the full sequence.
moves.bestUse: Confirm the first hit through an M1 string, wall combo or Glimpse of Power.

moves.name: Airborne Virgule Cascade
moves.description: Using Virgule Cascade after an uppercut or wall launch turns it into an aerial slash sequence.
moves.bestUse: Position the opponent near a wall, finish Jun's wall combo and activate Virgule Cascade as the target is launched.

moves.name: Glimpse Cascade
moves.description: Jun transitions from Glimpse of Power into a unique martial-arts and dagger sequence instead of the standard Virgule Cascade.
moves.bestUse: Keep both moves available when preparing this route rather than spending them separately.

moves.name: Jun Wall Sequence
moves.description: Jun pins the opponent against the wall, follows with close strikes and finishes with an upward sword slash that launches them.
moves.bestUse: Use the launch to confirm Airborne Virgule Cascade or dash forward for continued M1 pressure.

moves.name: No More Games
moves.description: Jun draws his sword and replaces his base kit with four cinematic burst and counter abilities.
moves.bestUse: Activate after creating distance or knocking the opponent away so the transformation cannot be interrupted.

moves.name: Savage Fangs
moves.guard: Approaching grab blockable when used raw
moves.description: Jun opens with sword slashes and transitions into a long cinematic attack when the grab connects.
moves.bestUse: Confirm it after the opponent spends their evasive or is recovering from a knockdown.

moves.name: Phantom Sever
moves.description: Jun rushes forward and performs a rapid displacement slash that can be redirected with its follow-up activation.
moves.bestUse: Punish movement, close distance or force defense before another awakening move.

moves.name: Piercing Tempest
moves.description: Jun throws a spread of knives. A successful hit causes him to teleport around the target with a multi-angle barrage.
moves.bestUse: Throw it after a knockdown, wall interaction or forced defensive action, not at a stationary blocking opponent.

moves.name: One Inch Punch
moves.description: Jun enters a counter stance and retaliates with a powerful close-range strike when attacked.
moves.bestUse: Read a predictable dash, M1 attack or immediate retaliation. Using it during Piercing Tempest converts the sequence into a combined finisher.

moves.name: Reliable Ground Combo
moves.input: 3 M1, downslam, Fang Dance, dash, 3 M1, Glimpse, Virgule Cascade
moves.description: The downslam confirms Fang Dance, while the Glimpse-to-Virgule transition uses Jun's strongest base variant.
moves.tip: Track the opponent's evasive before committing the second half of the route.

moves.name: Wall Variant Route
moves.input: 3 M1, downslam near wall, wall combo, Airborne Virgule Cascade
moves.description: The wall launch creates the height needed for the airborne Virgule variant and avoids using the move as an unsafe raw opener.

moves.name: Awakening Variant Route
moves.input: Piercing Tempest hit, then One Inch Punch
moves.description: A confirmed knife hit starts Piercing Tempest and lets One Inch Punch replace its standard ending with the combined variant.

moves.name: Resource Management
moves.description: Keep Glimpse of Power and Virgule Cascade together for their combined variant. Confirm Fang Dance before using it, preserve Wolf Fang Flash as a chase option and avoid spending all four base moves during one unconfirmed attack.

legendsBattlegroundsShinjiGuide.title: Legends Battlegrounds Shinji Guide
legendsBattlegroundsShinjiGuide.subtitle: Master Shinji's nunchuck pressure, lightning movement and Flashstep Fury reset mechanic. Learn how his aggressive playstyle compares with Martial Legend and Jun.
legendsBattlegroundsShinjiGuide.intro: Shinji is a fast pressure fighter whose base kit combines nunchuck barrages, electric movement and repeated Flashstep Fury attacks. He is available through Early Access, while the official FREE SHINJI event is scheduled to make the character publicly available on a date announced by the developers.

cards.name: Shinji Access
cards.description: Shinji can be selected through the Early Access gamepass. The official FREE SHINJI Roblox event will mark his wider public release.

cards.name: Nunchuck M1 Chain
cards.description: Shinji's basic chain combines punches, a multi-hit figure-eight nunchuck swing and a spinning hook kick.
cards.bestUse: Stop at three attacks when preparing an uppercut or downslam, then confirm a special move.

cards.name: Lightning Rage
cards.description: When Flashstep Fury is unavailable, landing Nunchuck Flurry or Body Flicker can refresh it once. A separate passive meter must recharge before another reset becomes available.
cards.bestUse: Spend Flashstep Fury first, trigger the reset with another move and use the refreshed Fury to continue pressure.

cards.name: Nunchuck Flurry
cards.description: Shinji performs a rising multi-hit nunchuck barrage followed by a downward strike. It can catch grounded targets and multiple nearby opponents.
cards.bestUse: Confirm it after a downslam and use it to activate Lightning Rage's Flashstep Fury refresh.

cards.name: Body Flicker
cards.description: Shinji crouches before dashing through nearby targets with electricity and displacing them to a new position.
cards.bestUse: Use it as a pressure reset or group attack after forcing the opponent to stop blocking.
cards.risk: A missed dash or awkward landing can leave Shinji open to punishment.

cards.name: Lightning Flash
cards.description: Shinji launches the target, disappears through a sequence of flash-step kicks and finishes with a powerful dropkick.
cards.bestUse: Shinji's main answer to blocking opponents or as a grounded-target punish.

cards.name: Flashstep Fury
cards.description: Shinji rapidly circles the target with a multi-hit electric barrage. It can strike grounded opponents and several enemies in the area.
cards.bestUse: Spend it early in a confirmed route, then refresh it through Lightning Rage for a second use.

cards.name: Electric Wall Sequence
cards.description: Shinji pins the opponent, delivers a rapid electric punch sequence and ends with a spinning kick.
cards.bestUse: Follow the wall sequence with Lightning Flash for a stable hit or Body Flicker to continue pressure.

cards.name: Warrior's Grief
cards.description: Shinji releases an electric wave during his transformation and gains large-scale lightning attacks for burst damage and team fights.
cards.bestUse: The activation wave can interrupt nearby opponents and create space for the first awakening move.

cards.name: Iron Onslaught
cards.description: Shinji lunges forward and starts a long single-target nunchuck and lightning sequence when the opening attack connects.
cards.bestUse: Confirm it after a stun, knockdown or evasive use.

cards.name: Lightning Rampage
cards.description: Shinji charges an electric shockwave, rushes through the target in a zigzag pattern and finishes with an aerial barrage.
cards.bestUse: Use the charge after creating distance or when several opponents are already recovering.

cards.name: Draco Meteor
cards.description: Shinji launches into the air with an electric dragon effect, selects a landing point and crashes down with a large area explosion.
cards.bestUse: Aim the direct impact at grouped or knocked-down opponents for stronger damage than the outer blast.

cards.name: Flashstep Reset Loop
cards.input: Front dash, 3 M1, downslam, Flashstep Fury, downslam, Nunchuck Flurry, refreshed Flashstep Fury, Lightning Flash
cards.description: This route spends Flashstep Fury before Nunchuck Flurry activates Lightning Rage and restores Fury for another pressure sequence.

cards.name: Safer Confirm Route
cards.input: 3 M1, downslam, Nunchuck Flurry, track landing, Lightning Flash
cards.description: A shorter route that preserves Flashstep Fury as a chase tool in case the opponent escapes.

cards.name: Wall Pressure Route
cards.input: M1 knockback, wall combo, Lightning Flash, dash chase
cards.description: The wall sequence stabilizes Lightning Flash and leaves Shinji close enough to chase the target's landing.

cards.name: Shinji vs Martial Legend
cards.description: Shinji brings faster chase, stronger multi-target pressure and repeatable Flashstep Fury offense, while Martial Legend has more stable counter-based control.
cards.recommendation: Choose Shinji for aggressive movement and team-fight pressure.

cards.name: Shinji vs Jun
cards.description: Shinji offers more mobility and faster pressure resets, while Jun has more unblockable options, confirmed move variants and stronger cinematic burst routes.
cards.recommendation: Choose Shinji for constant pressure; choose Jun for technical confirms and punish damage.

cards.name: Pressure Without Overcommitting
cards.description: Most of Shinji's base specials can be blocked, so alternate dash pressure, M1 attacks and Lightning Flash instead of using every ability in sequence. Preserve either Body Flicker or Flashstep Fury to chase an evasive.

legendsBattlegroundsGamepasses.title: Legends Battlegrounds Gamepasses and Cosmetics
legendsBattlegroundsGamepasses.subtitle: Compare character-access passes, private-server tools and cosmetic upgrades. See which purchases unlock gameplay content and which only change appearance or social features.
legendsBattlegroundsGamepasses.intro: Legends Battlegrounds separates character access from combat power. Early Access unlocks developing fighters, while most other passes add private-server controls, emote capacity, custom effects, outfits or profile features without increasing public-match damage.

items.name: Early Access
items.effect: Unlocks characters still in their Early Access development period, including Shinji before his public release.

items.name: Private Server+
items.effect: Adds practice and administration settings such as disabling cooldowns, granting awakening, forcing finishers, enabling dual movesets and managing players.
items.impact: Changes training and custom private-server sessions

items.name: VIP
items.effect: Adds a customizable cape, VIP title, optional arrival message, kill-count hiding, VIP server access and a discount on Gold purchases.

items.name: Awakening Outfit
items.effect: Lets the player assign a saved Roblox outfit that appears while their character is awakened.

items.name: Extra Emote Slots
items.effect: Expands the equipped emote wheel from four slots to eight.

items.name: Skill Text
items.effect: Adds customizable text bubbles or messages to selected skills and awakening actions.
items.impact: Changes how attacks are presented to others

items.name: Kill Sound
items.effect: Plays a selected custom sound after the player earns an elimination.

items.name: Kill Message+
items.effect: Displays a customized message when the player earns an elimination.

items.name: Standard Emotes
items.effect: Emotes can be unlocked, equipped to the emote wheel and opened in a match by pressing B.

items.name: Limited Emotes
items.effect: Special emotes are released through selected updates and limited events.

items.name: Cosmetic Effects
items.effect: Visual effects, accessories and character presentation options unlocked through the in-game cosmetic system.

items.name: Titles
items.effect: Displays an equipped title with the player's in-game identity.

legendsBattlegroundsUpdates.title: Legends Battlegrounds Updates and Sneak Peeks
legendsBattlegroundsUpdates.subtitle: Follow new characters, awakening moves, balance adjustments, cosmetics and developer previews. Each timeline entry tracks official Roblox and developer channel updates.
legendsBattlegroundsUpdates.intro: Arts of Vermillion and developer Zalekoth publish upcoming Legends Battlegrounds content through the Roblox community, official YouTube channel and X account. The timeline below covers major character releases, combat additions, cosmetics and current development previews.

entries.title: New Duels Lobby Preview
entries.summary: A new official sneak peek presents the redesigned lobby being prepared for Duels.
changes: New lobby environment for duel-focused play
Updated presentation for entering and organizing duels
Continued development of the competitive match experience

entries.title: FREE SHINJI Event
entries.summary: The official Roblox event will make Shinji available beyond his Early Access period.
changes: Public Shinji access event
Shinji-focused update content
Official event page for schedule and launch details

entries.title: Shinji Awakening Expansion
entries.summary: Shinji's Warrior's Grief content expanded his lightning-based awakening and large-scale pressure options.
changes: Iron Onslaught cinematic attack
Lightning Rampage charge and rush sequence
Draco Meteor aimed area attack
Additional anti-team combat systems
New limited emotes and visual content

entries.title: Jun Public Release and Shinji Early Access
entries.summary: Jun became publicly playable while Shinji entered Early Access with a complete speed-focused base moveset.
changes: Jun moved from Early Access to public availability
Shinji added with Nunchuck Flurry, Body Flicker, Lightning Flash and Flashstep Fury
Jun received Piercing Tempest and One Inch Punch
Skill Text customization gamepass added
Additional server, killstreak and competitive-play features

entries.title: Piercing Tempest Ending Sneak Peek
entries.summary: The official preview showed the ending sequence for Jun's Piercing Tempest awakening move.
changes: Ranged knife opening
Multi-angle teleport barrage
Expanded cinematic ending for Jun's awakening kit

entries.title: Jun Finishers, VIP and Private Server Tools
entries.summary: The update expanded Jun's moves, added finishing animations and introduced major customization and private-server features.
changes: Phantom Sever added to Jun's awakening
Finishers added for Jun's base attacks
VIP cape, title and Gold-purchase benefits
Private Server+ commands and dual-moveset settings
Expanded emote collection
Wall-combo, ragdoll and downslam adjustments

entries.title: Virgule Cascade and Cosmetic System
entries.summary: Jun received a new dagger move with multiple variants alongside the game's expanded cosmetic progression system.
changes: Virgule Cascade added to Jun's base kit
Normal, uppercut and Glimpse of Power variants
No More Games awakening and Savage Fangs
Gold-based cosmetics and additional emotes
Awakening Outfit gamepass
Photosensitive mode and shift-lock offset options

entries.title: Fang Dance and Jun Wall Combo
entries.summary: Jun's early moveset expanded with a new rush attack, environmental combo and first dedicated finisher.
changes: Fang Dance added
Jun wall combo introduced
First Jun finishing animation
Additional Martial Legend awakening content

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Legends Battlegrounds

questions.question: What is Legends Battlegrounds?
questions.answer: Legends Battlegrounds is a fast-paced Roblox stickman PvP fighting game made by Arts of Vermillion. It focuses on dashes, blocking, ragdoll escapes, awakenings and character-specific combos across free and Early Access fighters.

questions.question: Is Legends Battlegrounds free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Legends Battlegrounds is free to play on Roblox. Optional gamepasses such as Early Access, VIP and Private Server+ add characters, cosmetics and private-server tools, but they do not increase public-match damage.

questions.question: Which Legends Battlegrounds character is best for beginners?
questions.answer: Martial Legend is the best starting character in Legends Battlegrounds. The kit is balanced and easy to read, with a reliable counter, a guard break and simple combo extenders that teach neutral play before moving to Jun or Shinji.

questions.question: How do I unlock Shinji in Legends Battlegrounds?
questions.answer: Shinji is available through the Early Access gamepass in Legends Battlegrounds. The official FREE SHINJI Roblox event is also scheduled to release him publicly on a date announced by the developers.

questions.question: How do I activate awakening in Legends Battlegrounds?
questions.answer: Press G when your awakening bar is full to activate awakening in Legends Battlegrounds. Create space with a knockback first so opponents cannot interrupt the startup, then use awakening moves to secure the fight.

questions.question: What is the best combo to learn first in Legends Battlegrounds?
questions.answer: Start with the Martial Legend basic launch route in Legends Battlegrounds: Forward Dash, 3 M1, Uppercut, Panther Combo, Dragon Rush, Forward Dash, 3 M1, Uppercut, Tiger Palm. It teaches launcher timing and evasive awareness before harder routes.

questions.question: Are there redeem codes in Legends Battlegrounds?
questions.answer: Legends Battlegrounds does not currently use a redeem-code system. Characters, cosmetics and private-server tools are unlocked through gamepasses and in-game progression instead of codes.

questions.question: Where can I play Legends Battlegrounds?
questions.answer: You can play Legends Battlegrounds on Roblox across PC, mobile, tablet and console. The official game page and the Arts of Vermillion community are the primary entry points.

cta.title: Ready to Master Legends Battlegrounds?
cta.description: Join the Legends Battlegrounds community for character guides, combo tips, tier lists, and the latest Roblox updates from Arts of Vermillion!
